返回本批次

马自达可能已经通过 Skyactiv-Z 找到了 ICE 设计的巅峰

Mazda may have found the apex in ICE design with the Skyactiv-Z

newatlas.com 作者 breve 分数 46 评论 10 2026/03/26 17:38
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 尽管受到批评并正在走向电气化，但内燃机尚未消亡。尽管其乘用车设计可能已经开始通过马自达的 Skyactiv 设计达到顶峰。虽然被业界忽视，但 Skyactiv 发动机已逐步改进了内燃机 (ICE) 的各个方面，并且无需昂贵的涡轮增压或降低效率的再燃烧。展望未来，发动机将成为空气净化器而不是污染者。汽油与柴油汽油动力发动机存在热效率和燃烧率问题。由于火花点火，它们在可变功率输出和燃烧控制方面非常高效。但它们的热效率低且污染严重。柴油发动机非常擅长以较少的热量产生动力，并且能够以可预测的速率产生动力（扭矩）。但它们在可变功率输出方面效率低下，排放也很糟糕。将两者结合起来将成为发动机设计的圣杯，但这样做却存在问题。最初的尝试通常会遇到爆震（不可预测的点火）或其他控制问题。发动机中的堆积物也常常是一个问题。挑战主要在于汽油机和柴油机设计之间的巨大差异；特别是压缩比和燃料混合物。汽油发动机通常以大约 14.7 份空气与一份燃料的空气-燃料混合物运行。柴油通常运行得更稀，在 25:1 到 40:1 之间，具体取决于压缩比。这是两个引擎之间的另一个很大的区别。汽油发动机的运行压缩比通常约为典型柴油发动机的一半。汽油发动机设计的峰值压缩比通常约为 12:1，而许多柴油发动机的峰值压缩比为 25:1。压缩比是关键，因为压缩比越高，发动机将燃烧转化为曲轴上的压力（运动）而不是热量的效率就越高。HCCI 在以前的高压缩汽油发动机设计中，柴油与汽油的高压缩相结合，形成所谓的均质充量压缩点火（HCCI）。该设计还包括更稀薄的燃料混合物，以减少消耗与输出。然而，HCCI 面临一个主要问题：不可预测性。柴油发动机通过在气缸压缩冲程顶部喷射燃油来控制点火。但最后一毫秒的喷射意味着几乎没有燃料蒸发，因此燃料燃烧得很脏，而且通常不完全燃烧。如果以同样的方式注入汽油，情况会更糟。但是，由于发动机使用的差异，例如温度、压力和氧气与燃料混合物的差异，在进气冲程期间喷射汽化的汽油面临提前点火问题。因此，HCCI 下的冲程中自燃可能发生得太早或太晚。
  2. 第一款 Skyactiv 发动机是 -G，它比传统的 ICE 设计提高了燃油效率马自达
  3. 马自达 Skyactiv 推出马自达在 2000 年代初首次推出 Sky 和 ​​Mazda P 发动机系列，然后更新为更高压缩比的 Skyactiv-G（汽油）系列。柴油版本 (Skyactiv-D) 出现在多种型号中，并且在大多数情况下是相当标准的低压缩柴油发动机。尽管氮氧化物和颗粒物排放量要低得多。 Skyactiv-G 于 2010 年首次亮相，第一款车型用于马自达 Demio（又名马自达 2）。从 2013 年至今，马自达系列的其他产品均推出了更大排量的 -G 版本。 -G 还用于马自达制造的丰田 iA/Yaris。 Skyactiv-G 生产为直列四缸和直列六缸发动机。它们都是铝制的，压缩比范围为 13:1 至 14:1，排量范围为 1.3 至 3.3 升（1,298cc - 3,283cc）。除了较低压缩比的 2.5L Turbo 和 3.3L Turbo 之外，大多数都是自然吸气发动机。值得注意的是，这些早期的 Skyactiv 发动机没有 HCCI，这就是它们的压缩比相对较低的原因。 Skyactiv-G 的推出很快就产生了一种混合动力车型，称为 Skyactiv-Hybrid，它将 -G 与丰田的 Hybrid Synergy Drive 相结合。然而，在内部混合动力车完全开发出来之前，与丰田的短期许可就到期了。后来，它被称为 e-Skyactiv-G，这是一种轻度混合动力设计，用于较小的马自达车型，如 3、CX-30 和 CX-5。同样，马自达也尝试过压缩天然气 (CNG) 和旋转 (R) 版本的 Skyactiv 设计，但迄今为止都没有经过原型阶段。 Skyactiv-R 于 2023 年推出，作为电动或混合动力汽车的潜在增程发电机。最终，马自达找到了如何在较高压缩气缸中使用汽油来防止爆震（意外燃烧，又称爆炸）。工程师改变了燃料的喷射和点火方式。马自达将该系统称为火花控制压缩点火（SPCCI），用于其较新的 Skyactiv 发动机。与传统的汽油 ICE 设计一样，气体在进气冲程期间喷射以蒸发到进气中，但混合比例非常稀薄（大约 30:1 或更稀）。气缸循环的压缩部分将压缩推至稀混合气的近燃烧状态。然后，当火花塞点燃时，少量燃料会直接喷射到火花头处。这种预爆会导致压力突然增加，从而点燃气缸中的汽化燃料，使其同时燃烧。结果是燃料燃烧时没有局部富集（形成烟灰），整体燃烧温度较低（减少氮氧化物），并且热效率更高（使用的燃料更有力）。Skyactiv 系统的这一革命催生了 Skyactiv-X 发动机，它采用了 HCCI（称为 SPCCI）。该发动机将压缩比提高到 16:1，运行时比其前代发动机更加稀薄，燃油效率比以前提高了 20-30%。不使用涡轮增压器，但小型罗茨式增压器通过向发动机添加更多空气来扩大压缩点火窗口，以允许更稀的燃料混合——特别是在避免爆震更成问题的较高速度下。 Skyactiv-X 设计还允许标准汽油发动机在重负载（例如高速/扭矩情况）下以较低的压缩率运行。Skyactiv-X 应用于 Mazda3 和 CX-30 车型（2019 年至今），目前只有四缸发动机，但正在开发六缸发动机。Skyactiv-Z 与 -X 混合动力，但随着马自达宣布推出新款 Skyactiv-Z，情况可能会发生变化。这是从 -G 和 -X 到新的混合动力设计的又一次演变，可满足更高的排放标准。 -Z 是一款 2.5 升直列四缸发动机，针对 Euro 7、LEV4 和 Tier 4 法规，将在 2027 年 CX-5 Hybrid 中首次亮相。Skyactiv-Z 发动机比其前代发动机具有两大优势：由于增加了电动机功率，它可以使用更稀的燃料混合物运行，并且它包括隔热技术。这项技术将发动机运行中通常损失的热量转化为动力来运行同一台电机，从而提高效率。马自达尚未透露这是如何发生的，但这意味着可以在不损失功率的情况下满足更严格的废气排放（通常通过降低功率来降低发动机热输出来实现）。我们的猜测是，这将通过废热回收或热电发电来实现。也许是一种组合。
  4. 马自达每种 Skyactiv 汽油发动机设计的 BMEP 图表以及由此产生的逐渐提高的热效率
  5. 热效率已经成为 Skyactiv 发动机设计过程的重要组成部分。第一款 Skyactiv-G 在中等转速范围内具有良好的制动平均有效压力 (BMEP)，这直接导致更好的热效率。 Skyactiv-X 更进一步，扩大了有效 BMEP 的范围。马自达表示，Skyactiv-Z 的效率将远高于此。Skyactiv-Z 很可能首先作为强大的轻度混合动力或油电混合动力出现，然后再进一步实现电气化。马自达的工程师从一开始就将-Z设计为电动化，因此电动化程度相当灵活。 Skyactiv-Z 可以用作任何东西，从轻度混合动力的主推进装置一直到电池电动汽车的增程器。马自达表示，它还在试验碳中性燃料和基于沸石的排气管洗涤技术。这可以消除甚至成为净负二氧化碳结果——马自达的工程师看到了发动机产生负二氧化碳结果的未来。马自达的工程师们设想在不久的将来，内燃机的巅峰意味着它们不仅仅只是在道路和高速公路上驱动车辆。它们也可以是空气净化器，在运行过程中去除二氧化碳。这一结果不需要重大的基础设施改变，不需要对汽车设计进行根本性的改变，也不需要大量具有影响力的采购的新材料。这绝对是内燃机的顶点。

Original Summary

  1. Despite its critics and moves toward electrification, the internal combustion engine is not yet dead. Though its design for passenger vehicles may have begun to reach its apex with Mazda’s Skyactiv designs.Largely ignored by the industry, the Skyactiv engines have progressively improved everything about the internal combustion engine (ICE) and have done so without costly turbocharging or efficiency-killing reburns. And with a vision towards a future where engines become air purifiers rather than polluters.Gasoline vs DieselGasoline-powered engines have heat efficiency and burn rate problems. They’re highly efficient in terms of variable power output and combustion control thanks to spark ignition. But they are thermally inefficient and polluting.Diesel engines are extremely good at producing power with less heat and at producing power (torque) at a predictable rate. But they are inefficient for variable power output and terrible with emissions.Combining the two would make for a Holy Grail of engine design, but doing so has been problematic. Initial attempts usually ran into knock (unpredictable ignition) or other control issues. Buildup in the engine was also often a problem. The challenges lie mostly in the big difference between gasoline and diesel engine designs; specifically compression ratios and fuel mixtures.Gasoline engines typically run at an air-fuel mixture of about 14.7 parts air to one part fuel. Diesel usually runs much leaner, at anywhere from 25:1 to 40:1, depending on the compression ratio. Which is another big difference between the two engines. Gasoline engines generally run at about half the compression ratio that a typical diesel engine would. A gasoline engine design usually peaks compression at about 12:1 whereas many diesel engines peak at 25:1. Compression ratio is key as the higher it is, the more efficient the engine is at converting combustion into pressure on the crankshaft (motion) instead of heat.Enter HCCIIn previous high-compression gasoline engine designs, a combination of the high compression of diesel with gasoline would become what’s called Homogenous Charge Compression Ignition (HCCI). This design further includes a leaner fuel mixture to reduce consumption versus output.HCCI faces one major issue, however: unpredictability. Diesel engines control ignition by injecting fuel at the top of the cylinder compression stroke. But that last-milisecond injection means little fuel vaporization, so the fuel burns dirty and often not completely. Were gasoline injected the same way, it would be even worse. But gasoline that’s been injected to vaporize during the intake stroke faces pre-ignition problems due to variances in engine use, such as temperature, pressure, and oxygen:fuel mixtures. So spontaneous ignition can happen too early or too late in the stroke under HCCI.
  2. The first of the Skyactiv engines was the -G, which improved fuel efficiency over conventional ICE designsMazda
  3. Mazda’s Skyactiv is introducedMazda first introduced the Sky and Mazda P engine series in the early 2000s before updating to the higher compression Skyactiv-G (gasoline) series. The diesel version (Skyactiv-D) appears in several models and is, for the most part, a pretty standard low-compression diesel engine. Albeit with far lower NOx and particulate emissions. The Skyactiv-G was first unveiled in 2010 and the first rendition was used in the Mazda Demio (aka Mazda2).Larger displacement versions of the -G were introduced through the rest of the Mazda lineup from 2013 to present. The -G was also used in the Mazda-made Toyota iA/Yaris. The Skyactiv-G is produced as both inline four- and inline six-cylinder engines. They are all aluminum with compression ratios ranging from 13:1 to 14:1 and displacements ranging from 1.3 to 3.3 liters (1,298cc - 3,283cc). Most are naturally aspirated with the exception of the lower-compression 2.5L Turbo and 3.3L Turbo. It’s worth noting that these early Skyactiv engines did not have HCCI, which is why their compression ratios are relatively low.The Skyactiv-G’s introduction soon led to a hybrid model, called Skyactiv-Hybrid, which combined the -G with Toyota’s Hybrid Synergy Drive. Short-term licensing with Toyota, however, expired before an in-house hybrid was fully developed. That came later as the e-Skyactiv-G, a mild hybrid design used in smaller Mazda models such as the 3, CX-30, and CX-5. Similarly, Mazda has experimented with a compressed natural gas (CNG) and rotary (R) version of the Skyactiv design, neither going past prototype stage thus far. The Skyactiv-R was unveiled in 2023 as a potential range-extending generator for electric or hybrid-electric vehicles.Eventually, Mazda figured out how to prevent knock (unintended combustion, aka detonation) with gasoline in a higher compression cylinder. Engineers changed how fuel is both injected and ignited. Mazda calls this system, used in its newer Skyactiv engines, Spark Controlled Compression Ignition (SPCCI). As with conventional gasoline ICE designs, gas is injected during the intake stroke to vaporize into the intake air, but in a very lean mix (about 30:1 or leaner). The compression portion of the cylinder cycle pushes the compression to near-combustion at that lean mix. Then a tiny amount of fuel is injected right at the spark head as the plug ignites. This predetonation causes a sudden increase in pressure that ignites the vaporized fuel in the cylinder, burning it all simultaneously. The result is fuel burned without localized rich pockets (which form soot), at lower overall combustion temperature (which reduces NOx), and with higher thermal efficiency (more muscle for the fuel used).This revolution of the Skyactiv system begat the Skyactiv-X engine, which incorporates HCCI (as SPCCI). This engine boosts compression to 16:1 and operates far leaner than its predecessor and at fuel efficiencies that are 20-30% higher than before. No turbocharger is used, but a small Roots-type supercharger expands the compression ignition window by adding more air to the engine to allow leaner fuel mixes – especially at higher speeds where avoiding knock is more problematic. The Skyactiv-X design also allows standard gasoline engine operation at somewhat lower compression when under heavy load (e.g. high speed/torque situations).The Skyactiv-X is featured in the Mazda3 and CX-30 models (2019 model year to present) and is currently only a four-cylinder, but a six-cylinder is being developed.Skyactiv-Z hybridizes the -XThat may change, however, as Mazda has announced the new Skyactiv-Z. This is another evolution from the -G and -X into a new, hybrid design that meets much higher emissions standards. Aimed at Euro 7, LEV4, and Tier 4 regulations, the -Z is a 2.5-liter inline-four that will debut in the 2027 CX-5 Hybrid.The Skyactiv-Z engine has two big advantages over its predecessors: it can run on an even leaner fuel mix–thanks to adding electric motor power, and it includes heat insulation technology. This tech converts heat normally lost in engine operation into power to run the same motor that is improving efficiency. Mazda hasn’t said how that happens yet, but it would mean that meeting stricter exhaust gas emissions (usually done by lowering engine heat output by reducing power) could be done without power loss. Our guess is this will be achieved by either waste heat recovery or thermoelectric generation. Maybe a combination.
  4. Mazda's chart of the BMEP for each of its Skyactiv gasoline engine designs and the progressively improved thermal efficiency that resultsMazda
  5. Thermal efficiency has already been a large part of the Skyactiv engine design process. The first Skyactiv-G has a good Brake Mean Effective Pressure (BMEP) at middle RPM range, which directly results in better thermal efficiency. The Skyactiv-X took that a step further, broadening the range of effective BMEP. The Skyactiv-Z will be far more effective than that, Mazda says.The Skyactiv-Z will most likely appear first as a strong mild-hybrid or gasoline-electric hybrid before moving towards further electrification. Mazda’s engineers designed the -Z to be electrified from the get-go, so the amount of electrification is fairly flexible. It’s possible to use the Skyactiv-Z as anything from a main propulsion unit with mild hybrid all the way to a range-extender for a battery electric vehicle. Mazda says it is also experimenting with carbon-neutral fuels and zeolite-based exhaust pipe scrubbing technologies. This could eliminate or even become a net-negative carbon dioxide result – Mazda’s engineers see a future with engines producing a negative CO2 outcome.Engineers at Mazda are envisioning a near-future where the apex of internal combustion engines means they’re doing more than just propelling a vehicle around roads and highways. They’d also be air purifiers, removing CO2 as they go. An outcome that would require no significant infrastructure changes, no fundamental changes to automotive design, and without requiring a lot of new materials with impactful sourcing.That would definitely be the apex point for combustion engines.

评论（默认中文）

bob1029 2026/03/26 21:20
> 不使用涡轮增压器，但小型罗茨式增压器通过向发动机添加更多空气来扩大压缩点火窗口，以允许更稀薄的燃料混合物 - 特别是在更高的速度下，避免爆震更成问题。

考虑到效率目标，这是一个令人惊讶的工程决策。我很好奇他们如何设法抵消寄生损失。在 Hellcat 发动机中，增压器消耗 10 到 100 马力，具体取决于发动机负载。你永远无法将其关闭。

> No turbocharger is used, but a small Roots-type supercharger expands the compression ignition window by adding more air to the engine to allow leaner fuel mixes – especially at higher speeds where avoiding knock is more problematic.

This is a surprising engineering decision considering the efficiency goals. I'd be curious to see how they managed to offset the parasitic losses. In a hellcat engine, the supercharger consumes anywhere from 10 to 100hp depending on engine load. You can never turn it off.

在 HN 中查看
shmeeed 2026/03/26 22:32
Hellcat 使用大型双螺杆压缩机。罗茨式增压器是一种鼓风机，即一种没有内部压缩的正排量泵，可以非常高效地运行。马自达的装置相对较小且紧握，因此他们可以在不需要时将其关闭。
The Hellcat uses a massive twin-screw compressor. A Roots type supercharger is a blower, that is, a positive displacement pump with no internal compression that can run pretty efficient. Mazda's unit is comparatively small and clutched, so they can turn it off when not needed.
在 HN 中查看
fxtentacle 2026/03/26 22:12
“内燃机的巅峰意味着它们不仅仅是在道路和高速公路上推动车辆。它们还是空气净化器，在行驶过程中去除二氧化碳。”

这听起来很神奇，但后面是 0 个关于其如何运作的事实或信息。

“the apex of internal combustion engines means they’re doing more than just propelling a vehicle around roads and highways. They’d also be air purifiers, removing CO2 as they go.”

which sounds amazing, but is followed by 0 facts or information on how that might work.

在 HN 中查看
advisedwang 2026/03/27 00:58
他们正在将碳捕获技术应用到一些概念车和赛车中 [1]。系统中的更多信息位于 [2]。我认为这项技术在汽车中与在固定的大型设施中没有任何好处。

[1] https://www.hagerty.com/media/news/future-mazdas-to-capture-...

[2] https://www.carboncapturejournal.com/news/mazda-unveils-mobi...

They are putting Carbon Capture into some concept cars and a race car [1]. Some more info in the system at [2]. I don't see any benefit for this technology being in a car vs at a stationary large scale facility.

[1] https://www.hagerty.com/media/news/future-mazdas-to-capture-...

[2] https://www.carboncapturejournal.com/news/mazda-unveils-mobi...

在 HN 中查看
shmeeed 2026/03/26 22:43
这是令人恼火的营销废话，几十年来，汽车媒体一直在宣传每一代新一代高效发动机。 ICE（不使用氢气运行）总是产生二氧化碳。

如果反过来，那就是费托反应堆。 /秒

This is annoying marketing bs that the motor press has been touting for decades with every new generation of efficient engines. An ICE (that's not running on H2) always generates CO2.

If it did the reverse, it would be a Fischer-Tropsch reactor. /s

在 HN 中查看
linksnapzz 2026/03/27 00:42
在工作温度下，配备三效催化转化器的现代内燃机在适当的环境下（洛杉矶雾霾天）以高速公路速度行驶时，排气管中的氮氧化物和挥发性有机化合物水平很容易低于进入进气歧管的水平。
At operating temperatures, a modern ICE w/ a 3-way catalytic converter driving at highway speeds through the right environment (L.A. on a bad smog day) could easily have NOx and VOC levels at the tailpipe that are lower than what's going into the intake manifold.
在 HN 中查看
7v3x3n3sem9vv 2026/03/27 00:55
这就是福特在 2003 年左右营销其 PZEV（几乎零排放汽车）汽车的方式。 https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2004-jun-09-hy-neil9...
this is literally how Ford marketed their PZEV (practically zero emissions vehicle) cars around 2003. https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2004-jun-09-hy-neil9...
在 HN 中查看
shmeeed 2026/03/27 02:32
确实如此，而且已经有一段时间了。还是：氮氧化物和挥发性有机化合物，但不是二氧化碳。
That's true, and has been for quite some time. Still: NOx and VOC, but not CO2.

testing22321 2026/03/26 22:58
我相信有相当多的沃尔沃汽车在人口稠密的城市环境中使用时，从废气中排出的空气比吸入的空气更清洁。

https://www.volvocarsmississauga.com/en/news/view/how-does-t...

I believe there are quite a few Volvos that, when used in dense urban environments, emit cleaner air from the exhaust than they ingest.

https://www.volvocarsmississauga.com/en/news/view/how-does-t...

在 HN 中查看
shmeeed 2026/03/26 23:52
我确信他们喜欢人们相信这一点，但这是一个 机舱空气 净化系统，它涉及的是 PM 2.5 颗粒，而不是 CO2。

几年前，荷兰推出了一款电动汽车原型，其中确实包含主动二氧化碳捕获系统作为概念验证。

I'm sure they love people believing that, but it's a cabin air purification system, and it's about PM 2.5 particles, not CO2.

There was a dutch EV prototype a few years ago that did include an active CO2 capture system as a proof of concept.

在 HN 中查看
